name: strace
title: strace
summary: strace - trace system calls and signals
description:
System call tracer
strace is a system call tracer, i.e. a debugging tool which prints out
a trace of all the system calls made by another process/program.
The program to be traced need not be recompiled for this, so you can
use it on binaries for which you don't have source.
.
System calls and signals are events that happen at the user/kernel
interface. A close examination of this boundary is very useful for bug
isolation, sanity checking and attempting to capture race conditions.

command_examples: strace [-ACdffhikqqrtttTvVwxxyyYzZ] [-a column] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-o file]
strace -c [-dfwzZ] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-O overhead] [-p pid]... [-P path]...
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use strace because strace - trace system calls and signals.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use strace as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `strace [-ACdffhikqqrtttTvVwxxyyYzZ] [-a column] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-o file]` as documented upstream for strace.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `strace -c [-dfwzZ] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-O overhead] [-p pid]... [-P path]...` as documented upstream for strace.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `strace --tips[=format]` as documented upstream for strace.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair strace with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `strace --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: strace - trace system calls and signals
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install strace. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `strace [-ACdffhikqqrtttTvVwxxyyYzZ] [-a column] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-o file]`. Further invocations from upstream: `strace -c [-dfwzZ] [-b execve] [-e expr]... [-I n] [-O overhead] [-p pid]... [-P path]...`; `strace --tips[=format]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: strace - trace system calls and signals.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: strace - trace system calls and signals.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install strace
